codeReview.summary: The feature successfully resolved and proved the Linux/Node B frontend packaging path end to end. The npmDepsHash was correctly computed using prefetch-npm-deps, the Nix frontend build succeeded on x86_64-linux (Node B) on first attempt, and the public root at https://draft.choir-ip.com/ now serves the built SPA with real asset references instead of placeholder HTML.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The verification commands used by the worker (ssh node-b, nix build, nixos-rebuild switch, curl to public origin) are not captured in .factory/services.yaml. These are Node B deployment-specific commands that differ from local development commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows: 'ssh node-b "cd /opt/go-choir && nix build .#packages.x86_64-linux.frontend"', 'ssh node-b "cd /opt/go-choir && sudo nixos-rebuild switch --flake .#go-choir-b"', 'curl -sf https://draft.choir-ip.com/'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The library documentation now correctly documents the npmDepsHash resolution workflow, including the prefetch-npm-deps command and the pattern of using empty hash to get the correct hash from build errors (same as Go vendorHash).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/nixos-config.md contains: 'npmDepsHash — Hash of npm dependencies. Computed with nix run nixpkgs#prefetch-npm-deps -- frontend/package-lock.json' and '**Verified on x86_64-linux (Node B)**: The npmDepsHash = "sha256-UXHDem8sfIX42Aylef0AxtVWVjOI5gjIh5q0T41Qe5E=" was confirmed correct...'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the established pattern for Nix packaging: package-lock.json is checked into git for Nix reproducibility while pnpm-lock.yaml is used for local development. This dual-lockfile approach is now documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: flake.nix comment: 'Local development uses pnpm (pnpm-lock.yaml); the Nix build uses npm with a checked-in package-lock.json for reproducibility in the Nix sandbox.'

summary: This feature successfully completed the Node B frontend packaging verification. The npmDepsHash was resolved correctly on the first attempt using prefetch-npm-deps, and the x86_64-linux build on Node B succeeded without requiring hash adjustments. The deployment to draft.choir-ip.com now serves the real built SPA with hashed asset references (index-4UYezCQ3.js, index-D-d1h17n.css), fulfilling VAL-DEPLOY-006. All 5 services (auth, proxy, vmctl, gateway, sandbox) remain healthy post-deploy. The implementation correctly used buildNpmPackage with proper source filtering, PLAYWRIGHT_SKIP_BROWSER_DOWNLOAD to avoid sandbox issues, and documented the hash computation process in both flake.nix comments and library documentation.
